steps.title: Use the answer-choice structure
steps.content: "158% greater" means the mass is multiplied by $1+1.58=2.58$ each growth period. Since each period is $5$ weeks, the exponent must count 5-week periods: $t/5$.

steps.title: Eliminate by multiplier and exponent
steps.content: Choices A and C use multiplier $1.58$, so they represent a $58\%$ increase, not a $158\%$ increase. Choice B uses the correct multiplier but exponent $5/t$, which decreases as time increases. Only choice D has both $2.58$ and $t/5$.

steps.title: Quickly verify the starting value
steps.content:
Choice D says $M=36.55(2.58)^{t/5}$. At $t=15$, this gives:
$$36.55(2.58)^3\approx627.7$$
That matches the given mass.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $M = 538.92(1.58)^{5/t}$ — Uses multiplier 1.58 (not 2.58) and wrong exponent $\frac{5}{t}$. Eliminated.
  • B: $M = 457.66(2.58)^{5/t}$ — Correct multiplier but wrong exponent direction $\frac{5}{t}$; also wrong initial mass. Eliminated.
  • C: $M = 159.14(1.58)^{t/5}$ — Uses multiplier 1.58 instead of 2.58. Eliminated.
  • D: $M = 36.55(2.58)^{t/5}$ — Multiplier 2.58 and exponent $\frac{t}{5}$ are both correct, and $M_0 \approx 36.55$ matches our calculation. That matches choice D — D is correct.
